data.narrative:
NuScale Power operates in the nuclear energy sector as a public company, having raised over $600M across its funding history. The comparable universe consists of 11 peers total: 3 public companies (Oklo, Nano Nuclear Energy, and SMR) and 8 private firms including TerraPower, Kairos Power, X-Energy, and Nano Nuclear Energy.

The peer-median approach proved inapplicable to this valuation. The nuclear energy sector contains only 8 same-sector comparables, but the filtration for stage-equivalent, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier peers yielded fewer than the minimum 3 datapoints required for statistical reliability. Specifically, 2 same-stage peers and 0 adjacent-stage peers met criteria, and no similar-tier peers were identified. A broad all-sector median would introduce unacceptable methodological noise given nuclear energy's distinct capital intensity, regulatory framework, and commercialization timelines relative to broader energy technology markets.

This analysis does not support a triangulated valuation range. The dataset is insufficient to apply quantitative comparable-company methods with confidence. Valuation of NuScale would require either: (1) expansion of the peer set to include related infrastructure or regulated utility comparables, (2) acquisition of deeper financial metrics from the identified private peers (Oklo, TerraPower, Kairos Power, X-Energy), or (3) reliance on alternative approaches such as discounted cash flow analysis anchored to project-level capital requirements and regulatory approval timelines. The stage of commercialization and regulatory pathway clarity represent material uncertainties independent of peer-based methods.
